metadata.name: Tech Documentation Demo
metadata.description: Sample tech documentation for public demo

documents.title: Authentication Best Practices
documents.content: JWT tokens should be stored securely and rotated regularly. Use httpOnly cookies for web applications and secure storage for mobile apps. Implement proper token validation on every request. Consider using short-lived access tokens with refresh tokens for improved security.

documents.title: OAuth 2.0 Implementation Guide
documents.content: OAuth 2.0 provides a robust framework for authorization. The authorization code flow is recommended for web applications, while PKCE (Proof Key for Code Exchange) should be used for mobile and single-page apps. Always validate the state parameter to prevent CSRF attacks.

documents.title: Session Management
documents.content: Secure session management is crucial for application security. Implement session timeouts, regenerate session IDs after authentication, and use secure session storage mechanisms. Consider using sliding sessions for better user experience while maintaining security.

documents.title: API Key Security
documents.content: API keys should never be exposed in client-side code. Use environment variables and secure vaults for storage. Implement key rotation policies and monitor for unauthorized usage. Consider using scoped API keys with specific permissions rather than master keys.

documents.title: Multi-factor Authentication
documents.content: MFA adds an extra layer of security beyond passwords. Support TOTP authenticator apps, SMS codes, and hardware security keys for enterprise users. WebAuthn provides the strongest protection against phishing attacks.

documents.title: Rate Limiting Strategies
documents.content: Implement rate limiting to protect your API from abuse and ensure fair usage. Use sliding window algorithms for accurate rate limiting. Consider token bucket or leaky bucket algorithms for burst traffic handling. Return appropriate 429 responses with Retry-After headers.

documents.title: Vector Search Optimization
documents.content: Optimize vector search performance with HNSW indexes for approximate nearest neighbor queries. Tune ef_construction and M parameters based on your recall/latency requirements. Consider using quantization (PQ, IVF) for large-scale deployments.

documents.title: Hybrid Search Configuration
documents.content: Combine vector similarity search with keyword (BM25) search for optimal results. Tune the alpha parameter to balance semantic relevance and keyword matching. Use reciprocal rank fusion (RRF) or weighted combination for result merging.

documents.title: Cross-Encoder Reranking
documents.content: Use cross-encoder models for accurate relevance scoring on top candidates. Reranking improves precision at the cost of additional latency. Balance top_k candidates for reranking based on your latency budget. Consider caching rerank scores for frequently queried content.

documents.title: Embedding Model Selection
documents.content: Choose embedding models based on your domain and quality requirements. Voyage-3 and BGE offer strong general-purpose performance. Consider domain-specific fine-tuning for specialized content. Normalize embeddings for cosine similarity search.

documents.title: Request Tracing
documents.content: Enable distributed tracing to debug complex retrieval pipelines. Each trace captures embedding generation, vector search, reranking, and response formatting stages. Use trace IDs for correlating logs across services. Export traces to observability platforms like Jaeger or Datadog.

documents.title: Cost Management
documents.content: Monitor and optimize retrieval costs across embedding generation, vector operations, and LLM calls. Set budget limits per namespace or API key. Use caching to reduce redundant embedding generations. Consider tiered pricing with autopilot for cost-effective scaling.

documents.title: Answer Contract Validation
documents.content: Use answer contracts to validate that LLM responses meet quality requirements. Define contracts for sufficient evidence, factual grounding, and citation support. Failed contracts trigger fallback strategies like retrieving additional context.

documents.title: Namespace Organization
documents.content: Organize documents into namespaces for tenant isolation and efficient filtering. Use hierarchical namespaces like 'org:acme/project:docs/env:prod'. Each namespace maintains separate indexes for performance. Apply retention policies per namespace.

documents.title: Autopilot Configuration
documents.content: Configure Seizn Autopilot to automatically optimize retrieval strategies based on your latency and cost budgets. Autopilot dynamically adjusts top_k, reranking depth, and search mode. Monitor autopilot decisions through the dashboard for transparency.
